setupSteps.title: Get Your API Key
setupSteps.description: Go to console.velt.dev and copy your API key from the dashboard

setupSteps.title: Safelist Your Domain
setupSteps.description: Add app domain to Managed Domains in Velt Console

setupSteps.title: Initialize Velt
setupSteps.description: React: wrap with VeltProvider. Angular/Vue/HTML: call initVelt() or Velt.init()

setupSteps.title: Authenticate Users
setupSteps.description: Use authProvider on VeltProvider (recommended) or call identify(user, options) directly

setupSteps.title: Initialize Document
setupSteps.description: Call client.setDocuments() or setDocument() with a unique documentId and optional metadata

setupSteps.title: Install Velt Feature Components
setupSteps.description: Add VeltComments, VeltCommentTool, VeltPresence to enable comments and presence

setupSteps.title: Verify Setup
setupSteps.description: Run await Velt.getMetadata() in browser console to verify initialization

authProvider.description: Auth provider config with user, retryConfig, generateToken

apiMethods.description: Initialize the Velt client (Angular/Vue/non-React frameworks)
Initialize Velt in HTML/CDN usage
Authenticate the current user with Velt SDK
Initialize and subscribe to one or multiple documents
Initialize and subscribe to a single document

apiMethods.signature: Velt.setVeltAuthProvider({ user, retryConfig, generateToken, options?, onError? }): void
apiMethods.description: Configure auth provider for automatic token management (non-React)
Returns observable that fires true when Velt user and document are both initialized
Retrieve a one-time snapshot of debugging information about your Velt integration
Subscribe to real-time updates of debugging information
Subscribe to Velt client events (initUpdate, userUpdate, documentInit, error, etc.)
Subscribe to a folder and all or specific documents within it
Retrieve folder metadata and its subfolders with pagination support

hooks.name: useVeltClient
hooks.description: Access the Velt client instance in React components

hooks.description: Authenticate a user with Velt (React hook)

hooks.description: Initialize multiple documents at once (React hook)

authProviderShape.user: User object with userId, organizationId, name, email, photoUrl

behavioralNotes: SDK will not work without proper authentication and document initialization
In Next.js, add 'use client' directive at top of files containing Velt components
Call authentication and document setup in child components, never in the same file as VeltProvider
JWT tokens expire after 48 hours - use authProvider for automatic refresh, or manually re-identify on token_expired error
By default, authentication methods return null on failure; set throwError: true to throw instead
setDocuments() now filters out inaccessible documents instead of failing the entire operation
Users can subscribe to up to 30 documents at a time; first document is the root
setDocuments() with allDocuments flag and folderId retrieves max 50 documents
Angular: add CUSTOM_ELEMENTS_SCHEMA to NgModule to render Velt web components
Vue: set Vue.config.ignoredElements = [/velt-*/] to allow Velt custom elements

clientEvents.description: Fired when Velt user changes (login, logout, update)
Fired when a Velt Button is clicked
Permission provider events for access requests

commentModes.description: Pin comments on any elements or draw area comments
Add comments on table cells, like Google Sheets
Comments appear in a column on right side, like Google Docs
Leave comments as text highlights (enabled by default)
Leave comments at the page level via Comments Sidebar
Traditional inline comment style with section binding
Comments integrated into Tiptap rich text editor

Disable the triangle indicator in popover mode

setupSteps: Import TiptapVeltComments and add to editor extensions
Add comment button using addComment() from @veltdev/tiptap-velt-comments

notificationOptions.email: Configure SendGrid via Velt Console (Configurations -> Email Service). Requires: SendGrid API Key, Template ID, From Email Address
notificationOptions.webhooks: Trigger email notifications via webhooks for non-SendGrid services

behavioralNotes: VeltComments must be added at the root of the app (inside VeltProvider)
textMode is enabled by default; disable with textMode={false}
In popover mode, add data-velt-target-comment-element-id attribute to enable single comment per element
Without data-velt-target-comment-element-id, multiple Comment Annotations can be added on same element
In stream mode, set streamViewContainerId to the scrolling container ID for robust positioning
VeltInlineCommentsSection is multithreaded by default; set multiThread={false} for single thread
VeltCommentBubble and default triangle should not be used together to avoid visual issues
For Tiptap: disable default text mode (textMode={false}) to avoid double comment UI

settings.description: Enable notification settings UI for users

tabConfig.description: Customize tab names or disable tabs (forYou, documents, all)
Customize tab names or disable tabs

setupSteps.title: Enable Notifications in Velt Console
setupSteps.description: Go to Notifications section in Configurations in Velt Console and enable Notifications. Required before notifications will work.

setupSteps.title: Add Notifications Tool component
setupSteps.description: Place VeltNotificationsTool wherever you want the Notifications button to appear

setupSteps.title: (Optional) Embed Notifications Panel
setupSteps.description: If creating a dedicated notifications page, embed VeltNotificationsPanel directly

setupSteps.title: (Optional) Enable Notification Settings for Users
setupSteps.description: Enable in Console first, then enable via settings prop or API. Allows users to configure notification preferences.

setupSteps.title: (Optional) Configure Notification Delivery
setupSteps.description: Configure batching, delay, and digest in the Velt Console under API Key notification settings

apiMethods.description: Enable notification settings feature
Disable notification settings feature
Customize notification tab names or disable tabs (forYou, documents, all)

hooks.name: useNotificationSettings
hooks.description: Hook to get and update notification settings for current user

hooks.name: useNotificationUtils
hooks.description: Hook to access notification element for utility methods

hooks.name: useNotificationsData
hooks.description: Hook to access notifications data for current user; automatically updates when notifications change

hooks.name: useUnreadNotificationsCount
hooks.description: Hook to get count of unread notifications by tab

defaults.documentTabMaxCount: 15 notifications per 15 most recently active documents

behavioralNotes: Notifications will NOT work unless enabled first in Velt Console
VeltNotificationsPanel is automatically added/removed when using VeltNotificationsTool
In-app notifications are only generated and fetched for documents the user has access to
By default only latest 50 notifications fetched for For You tab
Notifications older than 15 days are not fetched by default
Notification settings currently only update settings for current user in current Velt document
When using multiple documents or folders, settings apply to the root document
Custom notifications can be added from your app via REST API

backendRestAPIs.description: Fetch CRDT data for a specific editor by editorId, or all editors in a document
Create new CRDT editor data on the backend (errors if editorId already exists)
Replace existing CRDT editor data with proper CRDT operations

behavioralNotes: Framework agnostic - use core library for any framework; React hook available for faster integration
Yjs ensures eventual consistency without merge conflicts (CRDT conflict-free sync)
Local-first reads/writes with automatic re-sync when back online (offline-first)
Same user can edit in multiple tabs simultaneously with full synchronization
editorId must be unique per editor instance, especially important with multiple editors
React hook cleanup is automatic on component unmount; non-React requires manual store.destroy()
Webhook debounce minimum is 5 seconds (5000ms)
Activity log debounce default is 10 minutes; minimum enforced is 10 seconds
CRDT webhooks are disabled by default
core-stores subdirectory (text, map, array, xml) was not found in the repository

name.description: Full name; defaults to random avatar name

photoUrl.description: Display picture URL; defaults to random avatar

color.description: Color for avatar border, cursor, selection

textColor.description: Text color when photoUrl not present

isAdmin.description: JWT token must also have isAdmin=true

accessRole.description: Set only via REST API, not frontend SDK

documentId.description: Unique document id generated from client document id

folderId.description: Subscribe to documents in given folder

allDocuments.description: Subscribe to all documents in folder (max 50)

context.description: Filter comments by custom context fields (AND logic)

id.description: Unique identifier for the collaborative store

debounceMs.description: Debounce interval in ms for batching local updates
